title: Embankment and cutting stability monitoring with InSAR
summary: Ground motion data reveals slow subsidence and slope movement along track before visible defects appear.
description:
Track-side embankments and cuttings can move slowly for months before visible track-geometry defects appear. Manual inspection is too sparse and slow to characterise movement across an entire corridor or to prioritise intervention.

The European Ground Motion Service provides millimetre-precision displacement time series derived from Sentinel-1, across the whole continent at regular grid spacing. Analysts can identify deformation hotspots, prioritise geotechnical inspection and intervene before failures develop.

site.integration_detail: EGMS displacement time series are used largely as delivered. Analysts threshold velocities and prioritise hotspots, but no heavy reprocessing of the InSAR data is required.
